title: While Loops and Loop Control Practice
instructions: Practice while loops, break, and continue statements. Write functions that use condition-based iteration.
hints: While loop condition should check if n > 0
Use break to exit loop when condition met
Use continue to skip to next iteration
Remember to update loop variable in while loops

testCases.description: countdown should return a list counting down from n to 1
countdown of 0 should return an empty list
find_first_divisible should return the first number divisible by divisor
find_first_divisible should return None when no divisible number exists
testCases.validation: assert find_first_divisible([1, 3, 7], 5) is None, 'find_first_divisible([1,3,7], 5) should return None'

testCases.description: sum_until_negative should sum values before the first negative number
skip_multiples_of_three should return numbers 1 to n excluding multiples of 3

description: Practice while loops, break, and continue statements to control iteration flow. Implement countdown sequences, early-exit searches with break, partial accumulation by stopping at a condition, and filtered iteration that skips unwanted values. These patterns are essential for condition-based looping in Python.
